headline: Guadalajara burns through 32,000 hectares
deck: Castilla-La Mancha moved the La Mierla fire into stabilization and began limited returns after almost 50 communities and 1,400 residents were affected. Crews still face wind and a vast perimeter.
body: Regional authorities put the La Mierla wildfire at roughly 32,000 hectares on Wednesday and moved the operation into a stabilization phase. Residents of several evacuated villages were allowed to begin returning, although officials warned that Thursday’s winds could complicate the line again. [E1]

Almost 50 small population centers and about 1,400 residents have been affected. The regional government began planning temporary water tanks and a recovery program before crews had fully controlled the fire, a measure of how quickly emergency response has become reconstruction. [E2][E1]

Associated Press reported that more than 200 firefighters were working with ground vehicles and aircraft on Wednesday. No serious injury or death had been reported in this fire. Thirty-four villages had been evacuated in the account, while the later regional tally included a wider set of affected settlements. [E3][E2]

Two days earlier the same blaze had already reached about 26,000 hectares and a perimeter near 120 kilometers. A national security update recorded state aircraft, forest-fire brigades and the Military Emergency Unit on the incident after the fire’s unfavorable expansion. [E4][E5]

Spain prepared early for a severe season. The government advanced its national campaign to 1 June after the first 15 weeks of the year produced 127 reported fires, more than three times the previous year’s count for the same period. Regional authorities still carry the first responsibility, with state crews layered on when an incident outruns local capacity. [E6]

European reserves add another layer, though they are spread across the continent rather than concentrated in Guadalajara. The 2026 pool includes 22 firefighting aircraft, five helicopters and 777 pre-positioned firefighters from 14 countries. Four countries had already activated the European mechanism this season. [E7]

Persistent heat and dry fuels raised the fire-danger setting across Spain, but the public record does not contain an attribution study for this ignition or its spread. Stabilization means the perimeter has stopped running away from crews. It does not mean the fire is out. [E8][E1]

evidence_box.fragment: aircraft, BRIF and military of the UME

art.title: The Sierra Norte fire line
art.caption: La Mierla burned into the Sierra Norte before crews reached stabilization · Map: Sprockett, Escalation Desk · Terrain: NOAA ETOPO1
